title: Portable Context Control Plane — Runtime Capabilities Input
description: Schema for the --runtime CLI input file. Describes tool availability at the current runtime. Class B input — if absent: treat as capabilityInventoryComplete: false, both lists empty; include all tool components; emit runtime_capabilities_missing warning. capabilityInventoryComplete governs fail-open vs defer logic: false = unknown availability = fail-open include for any tool absent from both lists; true = absence from both lists = confirmed unavailable = action: defer / path: runtime_unavailable. runtimeLabel is an opaque operator-supplied identifier string. Non-MVP fields capabilityTimestamp and capabilityVersion must NOT appear here (safe-defer 5-Q3/F-26). Provider/OpenClaw-specific fields must NOT appear here. Canonical owners: docs/06 §2.5; docs/11 §4.1; docs/12 §3.5.
$comment: Pass 4.9C-2B — Batch B. No code. No runtime. No provider/model calls. No docs edited. Non-MVP excluded: capabilityTimestamp, capabilityVersion (safe-defer 5-Q3/F-26). Canonical sources: docs/06 §2.5, docs/11 §4.1, docs/12 §3.5.

availableToolIds.title: Available tool IDs
availableToolIds.description: IDs of tool components confirmed available at this runtime. A tool ID present here is confirmed available and may be included in the plan. These are registry component IDs, not live tool endpoint identifiers.

unavailableToolIds.title: Unavailable tool IDs
unavailableToolIds.description: IDs of tool components confirmed unavailable at this runtime. A tool ID present here results in action: defer / path: runtime_unavailable in the tool selector. Must not claim token savings for runtime-unavailable deferrals. These IDs must not also appear in availableToolIds.

capabilityInventoryComplete.title: Capability inventory complete flag
capabilityInventoryComplete.description: Whether the availableToolIds and unavailableToolIds lists are exhaustive. true = absence from both lists means confirmed unavailable (same treatment as unavailableToolIds). false = absence from both lists means unknown availability — tool selector must fail open and include the tool component, emitting a runtime_capability_unknown warning. This field is required: true because its value governs fail-open vs defer split in the tool selector and runtime_capability selector.

runtimeLabel.title: Runtime label
runtimeLabel.description: Opaque operator-supplied string identifying this runtime configuration. Used for traceability only. Not interpreted by selector logic. Example: 'mvp_cli_static'. Non-MVP versioning and timestamp fields (capabilityTimestamp, capabilityVersion) are explicitly excluded — see safe-defer 5-Q3/F-26.
